problem_addressed: Deterministic embeddings from standard vision-language models struggle to capture uncertainties from ambiguities in visual/textual descriptions and multiple image-text correspondences, while existing probabilistic approaches require retraining VLMs with large datasets.
core_claim: GroVE provides a post-hoc approach to obtain probabilistic embeddings from frozen VLMs using Gaussian Process Latent Variable Models, achieving state-of-the-art uncertainty calibration across downstream tasks.
key_contributions: Proposes GroVE as a post-hoc method for uncertainty quantification in frozen VLMs
Develops a GPLVM-based framework with single-modal reconstruction and cross-modal alignment objectives
Demonstrates superior uncertainty calibration performance across cross-modal retrieval, VQA, and active learning tasks
novelty: Unlike prior probabilistic embedding methods that require retraining VLMs, GroVE works with frozen pre-trained models by learning a shared latent space that captures uncertainties without modifying the original VLM.
method_summary: GroVE builds on Gaussian Process Latent Variable Models to learn a low-dimensional latent space where image and text inputs are mapped to unified representations, optimized through single-modal embedding reconstruction and cross-modal alignment objectives.
method_components: Gaussian Process Latent Variable Model (GPLVM) for probabilistic embedding
key_innovation: Using GPLVM to model uncertainty in the latent space of frozen VLM embeddings, enabling uncertainty quantification without retraining the original model by capturing both latent space uncertainty and prediction noise.
assumptions: Frozen VLM embeddings provide meaningful initial representations
A low-dimensional latent space can capture essential structure and uncertainties
Single-modal reconstruction and cross-modal alignment objectives are sufficient for learning uncertainty-aware embeddings
theoretical_grounding: Relies on GPLVM's probabilistic framework for predictive distributions, where uncertainty in latent space increases variance of predictions through Σ̂∗ which accounts for both latent space uncertainty and inherent model noise.
headline_result: KL-Divergence achieves best performance with 0.79±0.02 accuracy on Image to Text COCO
stated_limitations: Deterministic embeddings from standard VLMs struggle to capture uncertainties arising from ambiguities in visual and textual descriptions and multiple possible correspondences between images and texts
Existing approaches for probabilistic embeddings require large datasets and don't leverage the powerful representations already learned by large-scale VLMs like CLIP
unstated_weaknesses: The post-hoc nature of the approach might limit the quality of probabilistic embeddings compared to models trained with probabilistic objectives from scratch
Computational efficiency and scalability of GPLVM for large datasets and models are not addressed
Domain mismatch issues when applying VLMs trained on general images to specialized domains like medical imaging
threats_to_validity: The approach might not generalize well to domains significantly different from the pre-training data of the VLMs
The quality of probabilistic embeddings depends on the quality of the original VLM representations
Uncertainty quantification might be less accurate for out-of-distribution samples
reproducibility: medium (code availability not mentioned in abstract)
open_questions: How well does this approach scale to very large datasets?
How does the quality of probabilistic embeddings compare to models trained with probabilistic objectives from scratch?
How does this approach perform with different types of VLMs or in different domains?
What are the computational requirements and feasibility for practical applications?
future_work: Not explicitly mentioned in the abstract
gaps_this_enables: Leveraging powerful pre-trained VLMs for uncertainty quantification in domains where fine-tuning is difficult or expensive
Obtaining calibrated uncertainties from deterministic models, which could be valuable for medical applications where uncertainty estimation is crucial
Applying probabilistic embeddings to active learning scenarios in medical imaging
relevance_to_topic: This paper is directly relevant to calibration-aware active learning for medical image classification as it provides a method for uncertainty quantification in vision-language models, which could be valuable for active learning scenarios in medical imaging.
can_run_on_macbook: unknown (computational requirements not specified in abstract)
reusable_assets: The GroVE method for probabilistic embeddings
The approach of using GPLVM for uncertainty quantification in frozen VLMs
best_result: KL-Divergence achieves best performance with 0.79±0.02 accuracy on Image to Text COCO
limitations_stated: Deterministic embeddings from standard VLMs struggle to capture uncertainties arising from ambiguities in visual and textual descriptions and multiple possible correspondences between images and texts
Existing approaches for probabilistic embeddings require large datasets and don't leverage the powerful representations already learned by large-scale VLMs like CLIP
title: Probabilistic Embeddings for Frozen Vision-Language Models: Uncertainty Quantification with Gaussian Process Latent Variable Models
